title: Response Time of a System: The System Time Constant
summary: The page concludes a discussion showing that system response is strongest when the input resembles a system's characteristic modes, and notes that exact matching leads to resonance-related behavior rather than a simple divergence. It then introduces subsection 2.6-2, defining the system time constant in terms of the effective width of the impulse response and motivating a rectangular-pulse approximation whose area matches h(t).
